challenge.name: Innovation Challenge on Generative AI Applications for Enterprise Scenarios Using OPEA

default_submission_fields.method_name: WearEdge Pro: OPEA Manufacturing Five-Agent Suite
default_submission_fields.method_description: WearEdge Pro is a wearable edge AI product packaged for the OPEA challenge as a Docker-runnable Manufacturing Agent Suite. The submitted repository starts an OPEA-style Gateway, Manufacturing Megaservice, Qdrant RAG profile, OPEA-compatible embedding microservice profile, official OPEA TEI embedding profile, OpenAI/OPEA-compatible LLM adapter, deterministic evaluators, guardrails, browser demo console, five route demos, a scorecard, and a lightweight GenAIEval-compatible evaluation pack. The Vuzix M400 / Android client is the real deployment front end and field-evidence source; the Web Demo Console at /demo is the judge-facing experience for reproducible evaluation. The five runnable agents cover maintenance / CMMS work order, IQC / QMS quality event, changeover / checklist verification, WI / released instruction guidance, and hazard / EHS observation. The official TEI profile has both local Docker Desktop and Google Cloud C3 c3-standard-4 fresh-clone evidence on a single-node 4-vCPU / 16-GiB-RAM / no-GPU configuration, which is inside the challenge limit of single node, <=64GB RAM, and 4-core CPU. The package includes 15/15 GenAIEval-compatible route evaluation cases passing, a champion risk burn-down, OPEA native depth matrix, CI-green upstream OPEA PR, contribution patch, public article, and demo video evidence.

do_not_claim_until_implemented.full official GenAIEval: The repository includes a lightweight GenAIEval-compatible dataset, config, runner, metrics, route evaluation JSON, benchmark JSON, and summary. Do not claim full official GenAIEval/RAGAS/AutoRAG/LLM-as-judge execution.
do_not_claim_until_implemented.production LLM service: The official OPEA TEI embedding path has local and GCP C3 fresh-clone evidence. The runtime now includes an OpenAI/OPEA-compatible LLM adapter and benchmark harness. Do not claim production LLM acceleration until a real endpoint benchmark records production_llm_endpoint_benchmarked with fallback_count=0.
do_not_claim_until_implemented.Intel hardware optimization: Google Cloud C3 Xeon AVX-512/AMX benchmark evidence is included for the deterministic five-agent route pipeline, and C3 Docker/Qdrant plus OPEA-compatible and official OPEA TEI embedding E2E runs validate the submission package. The Intel effective-use summary connects those workloads into an application-level effective-use record. Do not claim oneDNN/TEI microkernel dispatch proof or production LLM acceleration until those are separately benchmarked.
do_not_claim_until_implemented.OPEA public contribution: Public RFC issue, implementation comment, and official TEI update comment are posted at https://github.com/opea-project/GenAIExamples/issues/2461. A real upstream PR is open and CI-green at https://github.com/opea-project/GenAIExamples/pull/2462 from Ryanhuii:codex/wear-edge-manufacturing-suite-pr-ready to opea-project:main. DCO, pre-commit.ci, dependency-review, get-test-matrix, get-test-case, and compose-test all passed on head 0c149a06. A contribution package and git format-patch artifact are included at docs/opea-upstream/pr-ready.
do_not_claim_until_implemented.external public platform publication: The technical article is published on Dev.to at https://dev.to/ryan_hsu_wearedge/wearedge-pro-an-opea-manufacturing-five-agent-suite-for-frontline-operators-5afh and the demo video is published on YouTube at https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dd9k8m6PDco. GitHub article/video backups remain in the repository.
